summary: This comprehensive module covers Part V of the Indian Constitution, detailing the machinery of the Central Government. It integrates the executive authority (President, Vice-President, PM, Council of Ministers, and Attorney General) with the legislative authority of the Indian Parliament (Lok Sabha, Rajya Sabha, legislative processes, types of bills, and parliamentary committees).
keyHighlights: Union Executive: Articles 52 to 78 cover the constitutional head, executive leadership, cabinet supremacy, and the chief legal officer.
Union Legislature: Articles 79 to 122 define the Bicameral Parliament, officer roles (Speaker/Chairman), and parliamentary privileges.
Legislative Dynamics: Covers ordinary vs money bills, joint sittings (Article 108), budget procedure (Article 112), and key financial committees.
examFocus: Electoral College composition & Impeachment process of the President (Articles 54, 55, 61)
Comparison between Lok Sabha Speaker powers vs Rajya Sabha Chairman powers
Special powers of Rajya Sabha under Article 249 and Article 312
Differences between Money Bill (Article 110) and Financial Bills (Article 117)
Key Financial Committees: Public Accounts Committee, Estimates Committee, and Committee on Public Undertakings
quickIntroduction: The Indian Constitution establishes a parliamentary system modeled on Westminster principles. The Union Executive acts as the law-implementing arm, while the Parliament serves as the law-making body. Mastering their structural overlap and operational rules is essential for achieving a top score in SSC CGL General Awareness.
